scenario: Rails work session - audit the query Relation contract before a rework

map_quality.rationale: Names Rails as a multi-gem monorepo, places Relation in activerecord/lib/active_record/relation.rb, and lists the relation/ subdir modules plus creation entry points (core.rb:442, scoping/named.rb, querying.rb) and subclasses — the agent can pick an entry point with only small follow-ups.

specificity.rationale: Cites concrete paths and line numbers (relation.rb:1539, core.rb:442, scoping/named.rb:22/:154, querying.rb:24) and names each module file (query_methods.rb, spawn_methods.rb, delegation.rb, merger.rb) rather than vague areas.

justification.rationale: Calls Relation 'lazy, chainable' and notes spawn_methods does 'clone/merge' and querying delegates ~90 methods, but doesn't really explain WHY the interface is split into mixed-in modules or why chaining is deferred — mostly names the pieces.

uncertainty.rationale: No hedges or flags about pieces it didn't open (e.g. how delegation subclasses per model, what 'shared pieces it is built on' fully covers); reads as fully confident throughout.

relationship.rationale: States key relations — finder/scope/association *returns* a Relation, querying.rb *delegates* ~90 class methods to `all`, spawn_methods does *clone/merge*, AssociationRelation/CollectionProxy *subclass* it — but doesn't name that the query interface is *mixed in* from the relation/ modules, leaving that connection implicit.

steps.step: Map the Relation contract and what it is built on

map_quality.rationale: Names every required module (FinderMethods, Calculations, SpawnMethods, QueryMethods, Delegation) with file:line at relation.rb:68-70, plus the merger and value constants that the contract depends on. The agent could safely change the relation knowing what to preserve.

specificity.rationale: Concrete file:line throughout — `relation.rb:54-66` for value constants, `spawn_methods.rb:9/33-53`, `delegation.rb:23-69/138-147`, `merger.rb:7/43/52` — not 'the query modules'.

justification.rationale: Brief whys appear ('these define every chainable slot; the merger and assert_valid_keys depend on them', 'anything calling Relation.create/.new depends on this shape'), but doesn't explain WHY the interface is split into focused mixed-in modules or why delegation routes class methods.

uncertainty.rationale: No flagging of unconfirmed parts of the contract; nothing acknowledged as not opened or potentially incomplete (e.g. SignedId::RelationMethods, TokenFor::RelationMethods listed without caveat).

relationship.rationale: States the binding mechanism — `Includes` mixes the modules in at relation.rb:68-70, value constants drive merger/assert_valid_keys, `ClassMethods.create` ties delegation to per-model subclassing — but doesn't explicitly say which module supplies which slice of the query interface (e.g., QueryMethods supplies chainable where/order; SpawnMethods clones on chain) as a per-piece relation.

steps.step: Trace how a query is built up and executed

map_quality.rationale: The answer traces the full build-and-execute path with file:line at every layer: create (querying.rb:24, core.rb:442-443, delegation.rb:139), spawn (spawn_methods.rb:9-10, relation.rb:98-101), merge (merger.rb:17,58), compile (query_methods.rb:1673,1828; relation.rb:1243), and execute (relation.rb:1212→1436→1460→1492). An agent can follow this exact path to change query building safely.

specificity.rationale: Every layer is named with exact symbol + file:line ('spawn_methods.rb:9-10', 'merger.rb:17,58', 'relation.rb:1212/1436/1460/1492'), and key methods (`build_arel`, `exec_queries`, `_query_by_sql`, `instantiate_records`) are cited concretely rather than as 'the query code'.

justification.rationale: It implicitly explains spawning ('mutates @values on a fresh clone... dups @values and resets caches') and notes execution defers until `load`, but doesn't explicitly state WHY (immutability of an unexecuted relation) — the reader has to infer the laziness rationale.

uncertainty.rationale: No hedging or flagging of spawn/merge semantics or branches it didn't fully verify (e.g., the `apply_join_dependency` branch is mentioned as an alternative but not flagged as uncertain).

relationship.rationale: The path is presented as an explicit numbered chain (Create → Chain/spawn → Merge → Compile → Execute), with each arrow connecting layers (`current_scope.clone → Relation.create`, `spawn → clone → initialize_copy`, `load → exec_queries → exec_main_query`), making the route, not just the endpoints, legible.

steps.step: Audit every dependent of the Relation contract

map_quality.rationale: Exhaustive grouped enumeration covering all rubric-named areas: interface modules, spawn/merge type tests, connection role/shard, SQL bind handling (sanitization === Relation), cache keys, encryption rewriting, signed_id, eager-load joins, named-scope, association subclasses, AND the cross-gem dependents (railties query command, ActiveSupport enumerable mirror). Agent could act with only small follow-up lookups.

specificity.rationale: Concrete file:line throughout (e.g. 'sanitization.rb:214 — ActiveRecord::Relation === value', 'enumerable.rb:145 (pluck), :161 (pick)', 'query_command.rb:174-182') with a one-phrase use per site. No vague 'query helpers' language.

justification.rationale: Each dependent has a short phrase explaining how it uses the relation (inlines SQL, rewrites where-values, renders the relation, mirrors a method), giving WHY-by-area. Less prose on overall grouping rationale but the per-item phrases carry it.

uncertainty.rationale: Only the enumerable note hints at the static-search blind spot ('found only by name parity'); does not otherwise flag that short-name or ===-reached dependents could be missed or that the list should be verified.

relationship.rationale: States the connecting relationship per dependent — `is_a?(Relation)` dispatch, `=== value` type test, `< Relation` subclass, RelationMethods extension, `case … when ActiveRecord::Relation` render, name-parity mirror — exactly the chain-correctness signal the rubric calls for, including the cross-gem and unqualified cases.

steps.step: Trace how relation behavior is reused for associations

map_quality.rationale: Hands the agent file:line for the full reuse path: CollectionProxy/AssociationRelation subclassing Relation, Association#scope building it via target_scope.merge!(association_scope), AssociationScope#add_constraints assembling joins/wheres, plus the disable_joins variant. Agent could change relation behavior knowing exactly where the association path inherits it.

specificity.rationale: Every piece is named concretely with file:line (association_relation.rb:4, collection_proxy.rb:31, association.rb:107-115/315, association_scope.rb:21/124, relation/delegation.rb:7-14) — no 'the association code' hand-waving.

justification.rationale: Explains the WHY tersely — 'is a relation by subclassing', delegating reads to a scope, spawn contract reused — but doesn't fully spell out why association collections are built on Relation (so they inherit the chainable query interface).

uncertainty.rationale: No hedging on subclassing vs delegation semantics it didn't fully open (e.g., exec_queries override implications, how the delegate subclasses are generated per model) — agent has nothing flagged to verify.

relationship.rationale: States the path as connected relations: CollectionProxy/AssociationRelation < Relation → Association#scope does target_scope.merge!(association_scope) and scope.spawn → AssociationScope#add_constraints assembles joins/wheres → delegation glue routes the four subclasses. The chain from association collection to relation is explicit, not a file list.

steps.step: Assess the blast radius of the contract change

map_quality.rationale: Answer enumerates dependents across every area the rubric asks for — spawn/merge/execute core, subclasses, type-tested call sites (sanitization, predicate_builder, statement_cache), feature modules (encryption, signed_id), and the cross-gem dependents (railties query_command, activesupport enumerable) — all with file:line and a risk ranking. Minor gap: join_dependency and connection_handling role/shard are not explicitly named.

specificity.rationale: Every entry has file:line plus the exact symbol (`Relation.create`, `relation_delegate_class`, `build_arel`, `exec_main_query`, `query_command.rb:174`, `enumerable.rb:145,161`). No vague 'various helpers' anywhere.

justification.rationale: Each tier has a stated reason for risk (silent breakage via type test, class-identity drop, cache correctness, parity drift). Could go deeper on WHY signed_id/token_for are medium vs high, but the rationale is present per group.

uncertainty.rationale: Ranking is asserted with confidence; no explicit flag on the unqualified or cross-gem dependents being structurally-only surfaced or needing verification. The 'easy to forget' note on cross-gem is the closest thing.

relationship.rationale: Risk is tied to relationship: 'per-model subclassing via Relation.create', '`case when ActiveRecord::Relation`', 'override initialize/exec_queries', 'reaching in by short name / mixed into the contract', 'pluck/pick parity'. The route from the Relation change to each dependent is stated, not just labeled.

map_quality.rationale: Complete actionable map: contract files with line numbers (relation.rb:78/1212-1490/449-530, spawn_methods, merger, delegation, query_methods, finder_methods), dependents grouped by area including scattered call sites (sanitization, predicate_builder, statement_cache, database_statements) and cross-gem (railties query_command.rb:174, activesupport enumerable.rb:145/161), plus a thorough test list. A teammate could land the change from this.

specificity.rationale: Concrete file:line throughout — `core.rb:443`, `sanitization.rb:214`, `statement_cache.rb:138`, `integration.rb:163`, `railties/.../query_command.rb:174-182`, `activesupport/.../enumerable.rb:145`, and named test files like `relation/merging_test.rb`, `encryption/extended_deterministic_queries_test.rb`.

justification.rationale: Justification is mostly implicit through grouping (type tests / construction / sql_bind_handling / cache keys / association_subclasses) and a HIGH/MED/CROSS-GEM ranking with one-line reasons. Solid but could explain more explicitly WHY each test file belongs to which area of the contract change.

uncertainty.rationale: Notes section explicitly flags the riskiest unknowns: "Many dependents reach Relation without naming the full class... A class-name grep alone would miss these," calling out short-name refs, `.arel` callers, and cross-gem mirrors as the parts most likely to be missed.

relationship.rationale: Most files are connected via their relationship — type-test sites, bind/SQL handling, cache-key path, subclass overrides ("AssociationRelation subclasses Relation, overrides initialize/exec_queries"), cross-gem mirroring (`activesupport enumerable pluck/pick`), and the `creation_and_execution_order` chain. Test files are mostly listed by path without per-test relation back to which contract path each exercises, leaving a small gap.
